generation.image_prompt: Mystical 1900s lithography tarot card. A stormy rainy day. Three sharp silver swords are piercing a single leopard-spotted shield or heart-shape emblem. Heavy, dark clouds and lightning in the sky. Symbolic representation of deep grief and emotional pain. Heavy black ink outlines. Woodcut aesthetic. Full bleed. High-fidelity biological markers: narrow small-cat facial structure, distinct white stripes from nose to forehead, white muzzle, and large white spots behind black ears. Slender and agile feline build. Full bleed. No borders.
generation.narrative: The Three of Swords is 'The Grief'—the painful reality of the loss of core population members.

meanings.upright: Grief, heartbreak, emotional pain, sorrow, and betrayal.
meanings.reversed: Recovery, release, healing after pain, or repressing grief.

ecology.mapping_note: Three of Swords represents 'The Toll of Progress'—the emotional and demographic impact of losing breeding adults to roadkill.

website.excerpt: Under the stormy sky, the heart of the forest is pierced by three cold blades, mourning the fallen.
website.seo_description: Three of Swords reimagined as the grief of losing leopard cats in our modern landscape.

meaning.zh: Grief, heartbreak, emotional pain, sorrow, and betrayal.
meaning.en: Grief, heartbreak, emotional pain, sorrow, and betrayal.
